122.reason: [When values are equal but nodes are different (due to duplicates), this incorrectly assumes the target is in the right subtree without verifying. This can cause infinite recursion or wrong height calculation.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When values are equal but nodes are different (due to duplicates), this incorrectly assumes the target is in the right subtree without verifying. This can cause infinite recursion or wrong height calculation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When values are equal but nodes are different (e.g., duplicates), this incorrectly assumes the target is in the right subtree without verifying identity, leading to infinite recursion or wrong height calculation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [This recursive call assumes p is in the right subtree, but if duplicates exist and p is actually in the left subtree due to insertion logic inconsistency, this will fail. Also, comparing only by value without pointer equality can cause infinite recursion or wrong path traversal when duplicate values exist.]

Also, comparing by value alone can lead to incorrect traversal when duplicate values exist.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [When values are equal but nodes are different (e.g., duplicates), this incorrectly assumes the target is in the right subtree without verifying pointer equality first. This can cause infinite recursion or wrong height calculation.]
